title: EAFIX SSOT Registry System Delivery Verification Specification
purpose: Define a deterministic, fail-closed, evidence-bound verification contract that an independent agent can use to determine whether the final merged registry implementation plan delivered every required task, output, gate, authority transition, migration control, consumer, and operational safeguard correctly.

target_plan.binding_rule: A verification run is invalid if the target plan bytes, plan_id, or plan_version differ. Update this specification through governance rather than silently verifying another plan.

repository.target_commit_policy: Every verification run must bind to one immutable commit SHA.
repository.working_tree_policy: Evidence collection begins and ends with a clean working tree except inside isolated mutation or rollback fixtures.
repository.branch_policy: Verification may use read-only master plus disposable branches/worktrees; it must not push directly to the default branch.

routing_and_authority.routing_first_rule: Consult the routing authority before resolving any module, process, contract, file, MT4, channel, UI, calendar, capability, or governance claim.

required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

required_reference_documents.scope: current module identity and responsibility before cutover
required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

required_reference_documents.scope: current step identity and handoffs before cutover
required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

required_reference_documents.scope: current phases, validation and failure contracts before cutover
required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

required_reference_documents.scope: MT4-Python channels, protocols, ports, direction and owners
required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

required_reference_documents.scope: operator UI entities, behavior, controls and contracts
required_reference_documents.required_when: When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.
When the verification check touches this authority scope or a route selected by the target plan.

pre_cutover_priority: Current subject-specific canonical structured authority selected by routing/doc authority
Current specialized MT4, communication, UI, calendar, schema, and capability authorities
post_cutover_priority: selected canonical authored registry or registered external authority
routing_and_authority.conflict_rule: Current routed authority wins until a governed evidence-backed decision and atomic cutover explicitly changes the authority pointer.
routing_and_authority.missing_reference_rule: Record missing or stale references explicitly and block only conclusions or tasks that depend on them; never silently substitute a lower-authority source.

rules: This specification defines success; it does not store run verdicts.
The companion schema validates structure, not delivery results.
Generated derivatives are non-authoritative and must be regenerated from this specification plus the bound target plan.
Run-specific evidence and reports never modify this specification.
Any change to checks, gates, counts, binding, or verdict rules requires a new specification version and fresh verification.

verification_operating_model.principle: requirement -> expected deliverable -> verification method -> evidence -> verdict
verification_operating_model.independence_rule: The verifier must not rely on the implementing agent's summary as evidence; it must inspect repository state, artifacts, tests, CI, decisions, and hashes directly.
verification_operating_model.result_separation_rule: Specification, evidence, and run verdicts remain separate artifacts.
verification_operating_model.progression_rule: A gate may be evaluated only after all dependencies pass. Failure or blocked status in a blocker/high check stops downstream completion claims.
verification_operating_model.scope_rule: A finding may block only its dependent scope before cutover, but final verified_complete requires every target-plan definition-of-done obligation to pass.
verification_operating_model.reverification_rule: A target commit, target plan, specification, authority source, workflow, schema, or validated input change invalidates dependent evidence and requires re-execution.

plan_obligation_inventory.stable_obligation_id_rule: IDs derive only from source identity and ordinal/field location, never from requirement text or observed result.
plan_obligation_inventory.source_pointer_rule: Every obligation stores an RFC 6901 JSON Pointer to the bound target plan and the source value hash.
plan_obligation_inventory.multiple_mapping_rule: Multiple verification checks are permitted only when dimensions differ and the matrix records a rationale.
plan_obligation_inventory.count_change_rule: Any mismatch between extracted counts and expected_counts blocks generation and requires a governed specification or target-plan update.

allowed_verdicts.meaning: All required procedures and expected results are satisfied by fresh, resolvable evidence.
The check executed and one or more required expected results were not satisfied.
The check could not execute or conclude because a prerequisite, authority, environment, permission, or evidence source was unavailable.
The check is inapplicable to the delivered scope and carries an exact plan-based rationale plus required approval.

not_applicable_rules: The check applicability field must permit not_applicable.
The result must cite the exact plan obligation and rationale.
A blocker or high check requires owner approval for not_applicable.
not_applicable cannot be used to bypass a definition-of-done criterion.
verdict_taxonomy.blocked_rule: blocked is never equivalent to pass and prevents verified_complete for the affected scope.
verdict_taxonomy.warning_rule: Warnings are findings attached to a verdict; warning is not a verdict.

levels.completion_effect: Prevents affected task, phase, cutover, migration, or final completion.
Must pass for verified_complete unless an explicit plan-authorized not_applicable approval exists.
Requires disposition and owner assignment; may not contradict a completion criterion.
Does not block but remains visible in the report.

values.rule: Result is produced deterministically by a script, validator, test, or query.
Result requires explicit human inspection or owner decision and still produces identifiable evidence.
Automated evidence is combined with explicit human review or architectural judgement.

evidence_contract.hash_rule: Every file or payload evidence object must have SHA-256. External URIs also record immutable commit/run identifiers when available.
evidence_contract.command_rule: Command evidence records exact command, working directory, environment summary, start/end time, exit code, stdout path, and stderr path.
evidence_contract.manual_rule: Manual evidence records reviewer identity, reviewed artifacts/hashes, rationale, and explicit conclusion.
evidence_contract.reference_rule: Verification results refer to evidence_id values; unsupported prose is not evidence.

stale_triggers: A relevant authority or schema changes.
A relevant CI workflow changes after execution.
A validated input or generated artifact changes.
Evidence cannot be tied to an immutable commit, run, or hash.
freshness_contract.rule: Stale or unverifiable evidence cannot support pass.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Require integration evidence and consumer references for every output where integration is applicable.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: A required deliverable has no verified consumer, generator, validator, routing, or governance connection.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Regenerate and compare bytes; inspect provenance and managed-output manifest.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: Committed generated bytes differ from deterministic output.

anti_false_completion_controls.pattern: Validator exists but is not executed
anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Trace validator into CI, task commands, or verification transcripts.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: A required validator has no fresh execution evidence.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Inspect continue-on-error, report-only flags, required-check rules, and registry maturity.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: A canonical or completion-critical check can fail without blocking.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Inspect workflow run, jobs, steps, conclusions, and startup blockers.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: Skipped, neutral, cancelled, or startup-blocked evidence is presented as pass.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Compare evidence target_commit_sha and relevant input hashes to the verification context.
One-for-one reconcile baseline removals to resolution evidence and parity IDs.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: Any removal lacks a valid resolution record.

anti_false_completion_controls.pattern: Authority edited to make parity green
anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Inspect authority diffs during parity and burn-down PR classes.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: An authority edit is used to eliminate a registry divergence without separate governance approval.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Validate field-group ownership and active document authority state.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: More than one active source owns the same governed fact.

anti_false_completion_controls.pattern: Task marked complete without outputs or acceptance evidence
anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Join task completion records to output and acceptance obligations.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: Any task-complete record lacks all required outputs and acceptance evidence.

anti_false_completion_controls.pattern: Human decision inferred rather than recorded
anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Require identifiable approval record and decision registry reference.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: A blocking decision has no explicit approval.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Require executed rollback transcript and restored-state validation.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: An irreversible or migration action lacks successful rollback evidence.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Require a real downstream consumer and break-and-restore mutation proof.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: No required downstream consumer depends on registry data.

anti_false_completion_controls.pattern: Legacy authority remains active after cutover
anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Cross-check registry status, doc authority, routing, supersession pointers, and banners.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: A predecessor remains selected as current authority for a cut-over fact group.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Execute clean-clone build and compare committed/generated hashes.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: Hidden local state or uncommitted inputs are required.

anti_false_completion_controls.detection: Compare published wording to actual registry state, baseline, CI, migration scope, and check verdicts.
anti_false_completion_controls.blocking_when: The final status claims more than the evidence supports.

verification_gates.title: Input and scope integrity
verification_gates.purpose: Bind the run to the exact plan and repository state before implementation claims are examined.

checks.title: Bind verification to the exact final plan

requirement_sources.summary: Final merged plan identity and integrity

procedure: Compute SHA-256 of the target plan file.
Parse the plan and compare plan_id, plan_version, and document_type to the bound values in this specification.

required_evidence.description: Target plan identity, path, byte size, and SHA-256

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Change one byte in a temporary copy of the plan.
negative_test.expected_failure: Target-binding check exits nonzero and reports a hash mismatch.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Resolve mandatory routing and authority references

requirement_sources.summary: Missing references must not be silently substituted

procedure: Read the routing authority first.
Resolve every required reference from the plan against the target commit.
Record each reference as resolved, missing, stale, moved, ambiguous, or not applicable.
Block only conclusions and checks that depend on unresolved references.
expected_results: Every required reference has an explicit resolution status.
No lower-authority source is silently substituted.
The authority priority applied matches the target plan.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Freeze repository and verification context
procedure: Record repository full name, target branch, target commit SHA, default branch, ruleset state, required checks, submodules, and clean-tree status.
Record verifier tool versions and operating system.
expected_results: Target commit is immutable for the verification run.
Working tree is clean before evidence collection.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify source-plan consolidation inventory

requirement_sources.summary: Source inventory, hashes, duplicates, and supersession

procedure: Hash each source entry available in the repository or evidence bundle.
Verify the declared duplicate naming-plan relationship.
Verify remediation v1.1.0 supersedes v1.0.0 and the amendment is provenance only.
expected_results: No source plan remains an active sequencing dependency after final-plan approval.

required_evidence.description: Source file hashes, versions, IDs, and dispositions

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Remove one source-inventory row from a temporary generated matrix.
negative_test.expected_failure: Coverage validator reports the source entry as unmapped.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify verification specification schema conformance
procedure: Validate this specification against its companion JSON Schema using Draft 2020-12.
Run the semantic validation rules declared by this specification.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Delete a required field from a temporary copy of the verification specification.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify evidence storage is isolated and immutable by run

requirement_sources.summary: Evidence capture and final reporting requirements

procedure: Create a unique run_id and evidence root.
Prevent prior-run evidence from being overwritten.
Record hashes for every evidence object in the run manifest.
expected_results: No evidence object is referenced without a hash.
Evidence from other commits is marked stale and excluded.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify environmental blockers are explicit

requirement_sources.summary: Environmental blockers must not be counted as passes

procedure: Check CI account state, required tool availability, repository permissions, and platform constraints.
Classify unavailable execution as blocked, never pass.
expected_results: No skipped, neutral, startup-blocked, or unavailable check is recorded as passing.
All blocked checks name the blocker and affected scope.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Stop the verification run. Do not evaluate downstream delivery claims against an unbound or unresolved input set.

verification_gates.title: Plan coverage completeness
verification_gates.purpose: Prove that the verification package covers the complete final plan rather than a selected subset.

checks.title: Generate the complete plan-obligation inventory
procedure: Traverse the target plan using the obligation-generation rules in this specification.
Emit one obligation record per counted source element with exact JSON pointer and stable obligation_id.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Delete one generated obligation record from a temporary matrix.
negative_test.expected_failure: Coverage validator reports an exact count mismatch and the missing plan pointer.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify exact obligation category counts
procedure: Compare generated category counts to target-plan-derived expected counts bound in this specification.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Increment one expected category count in a temporary specification copy.
negative_test.expected_failure: Semantic validation reports a count mismatch.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Map every obligation to verification checks
procedure: Assign one or more verification_check_ids to every obligation.
Require explicit rationale when multiple checks cover one obligation.
expected_results: mapped_multiple_explained contains rationale for every occurrence
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Clear verification_check_ids for one obligation in a temporary matrix.
negative_test.expected_failure: Coverage validator exits nonzero and identifies the unmapped obligation.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify task acceptance clauses are not collapsed into task-level claims
procedure: Confirm each acceptance clause has its own obligation record and evidence mapping.
Reject a single generic task-complete record that does not enumerate acceptance clauses.
expected_results: Each acceptance obligation has at least one verification mapping.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify outputs are checked across required dimensions
procedure: For each output obligation, require applicable checks for existence, validity, provenance, integration, and enforcement.
Permit dimension not_applicable only with explicit rationale and approval policy.
expected_results: Every output has five dimension statuses.
No output passes on existence alone.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify human decisions and write scopes are fully mapped

requirement_sources.summary: Task write allowlists and forbidden paths

procedure: Generate one obligation per human decision and per write scope.
Map each blocking decision to approval evidence and each write scope to diff-scope verification.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify definition-of-done coverage
procedure: Generate one obligation for each definition-of-done criterion.
Map each criterion to one or more independent checks and evidence types.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify no excluded obligation classes
procedure: Compare extracted obligation classes to the complete generation-rule registry.
Reject undocumented exclusions.
expected_results: All 15 declared obligation classes are present.
Exclusion count == 0 unless approved by an explicit plan amendment.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Stop. A verification report with incomplete obligation coverage is invalid regardless of implementation quality.

verification_gates.title: Task and phase delivery
verification_gates.purpose: Verify that all planned work was executed in order, within scope, with outputs and evidence.

checks.title: Verify task dependency order
procedure: Build phase and task dependency graphs.
Verify every completed task has evidence that predecessors were complete before execution.
Detect cycles and missing references.

required_evidence.description: Execution ledger with timestamps and IDs

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Create a synthetic dependency cycle in a temporary plan fixture.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify every task has a completion record
procedure: Match every task_id to exactly one execution-ledger record.
Require status, commit/PR references, changed files, evidence IDs, and acceptance results.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify task preconditions and human gates
procedure: For each task, verify listed preconditions were true before execution.
For tasks or phases requiring human approval, verify approval predates the governed action.

required_evidence.description: Human approvals with decision IDs and timestamps

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify task actions and commands were executed or validly superseded

requirement_sources.summary: Current repository state governs historical command literals

procedure: Compare each task action and command to transcripts and changed artifacts.
When a historical literal was replaced, require an evidence-backed substitution record.
expected_results: Every required action is executed or governed as superseded.
Every command substitution cites live repository evidence.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify task outputs satisfy content and provenance requirements
procedure: Resolve every output path or artifact ID.
Validate content, schema, source linkage, generating task, commit, and hash.
Reject placeholder or empty outputs unless explicitly allowed.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify every acceptance clause has direct evidence
procedure: Evaluate each acceptance obligation independently.
Link each verdict to evidence IDs and prohibit unsupported inherited pass status.
expected_results: Acceptance clauses with pass and evidence == expected count or valid not_applicable.
Blocked acceptance clauses prevent affected task and phase completion.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify phase exit gates controlled progression

requirement_sources.summary: Phase exit gates and pass conditions

procedure: Verify each exit gate condition separately.
Confirm downstream phase work did not begin before predecessor gate approval.
Record gate decision and evidence.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify write-scope compliance for every task and PR

requirement_sources.summary: Every task obeys its write scope

procedure: For each task/PR, compare changed paths with the referenced write_scope_id.
Treat a forbidden or unlisted path as blocking unless an approved plan amendment exists.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Add an unlisted file path to a temporary changed-file fixture.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

verification_gates.on_fail: Mark the affected tasks and phases incomplete. Do not infer completion from later successful phases.

verification_gates.title: Artifact and registry integrity
verification_gates.purpose: Verify structural validity, provenance, managed outputs, registry graph integrity, and non-duplication of authority.

checks.title: Validate all authored registry files and schemas

requirement_sources.summary: Registry schema, ID, reference, and authority rules

procedure: Run all registry schema, ID, reference, and authority validators.
Capture exact record and error counts.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Inject a duplicate ID and invalid foreign reference into temporary fixtures.
negative_test.expected_failure: Relevant validators exit nonzero and identify registry, record, field, and target.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify all ten registries and generated current views

requirement_sources.summary: Ten authored registries and generated projections

procedure: Inventory authored registries, schemas, current views, graph, reports, and build manifest.
Verify authored and generated classifications.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify registry build manifest integrity
procedure: Verify every listed input and output hash.
Verify POSIX repository-relative keys.
Verify the manifest does not hash itself.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Alter one managed output byte in a temporary worktree.
negative_test.expected_failure: Manifest verification and --check both fail without rewriting the file.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify generated artifacts are non-authoritative and non-editable

requirement_sources.summary: Generated artifacts must not be hand edited

procedure: Compare generated files to deterministic render output.
Inspect authority records to confirm generated files are not active authored authorities.
Check repository protections or CI enforcement.
expected_results: Manual edit detection is blocking where required
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify stable IDs and cross-registry graph completeness
procedure: Validate stable ID formats, uniqueness, non-reuse, and cross-registry references.
Compare graph edges to declared relationships.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Create a temporary record that reuses an ID or points to a nonexistent target.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify registry completeness without hiding planned or unknown states

requirement_sources.summary: No unresolved incomplete states at completion

procedure: Inspect completeness, conflict, and orphan reports.
Verify planned modules use explicit planned status rather than fabricated implementation data.
Verify residual unknowns are visible and block only affected scope.
expected_results: Residual unknowns have scope and disposition
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify specialized schemas remain single-home authorities

requirement_sources.summary: One canonical schema home per contract shape

procedure: Inventory canonical cross-module schemas and module-local schema content.
Detect duplicate authoritative shapes.
Verify local content is reference, example, projection, or private schema.
expected_results: Every schema has one declared authority owner
Local duplicates are non-authoritative or rejected
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify operational process is a registry-backed projection

requirement_sources.summary: Operational process must not become a competing authority

procedure: Inspect process registry ownership and operational-process outputs.
Trace every projected fact to registry or registered external authority.
Verify no independently editable current process authority was introduced.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Reject completion for affected registry or artifact domains and preserve exact invalid records and paths.

verification_gates.title: Authority and governance
verification_gates.purpose: Prove the SSOT authority model, decisions, cutovers, routing, and supersession are correct and auditable.

checks.title: Verify one active canonical owner per governed fact group

requirement_sources.summary: Exactly one owner for each fact group

procedure: Validate authority manifest coverage and overlap rules.
Verify every governed fact group has exactly one active canonical source after applicable cutover.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Assign a second active owner to one fact group in a fixture.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify pre-cutover and post-cutover authority ordering
procedure: For each registry and fact group, determine cutover state at the target commit.
Apply the correct authority priority and verify routing/doc authority agree.
expected_results: No candidate overrides current authority before cutover.
No superseded authority remains selected after cutover.
Routing and doc authority are consistent.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify human decisions are authentic, timely, and scoped
procedure: Verify each blocking decision has an identifiable approver, decision ID, rationale, selected option, timestamp, and affected scope.
Verify approval predates irreversible action.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify source plans and duplicate documents are superseded correctly
procedure: Inspect doc authority, routing, banners, archive location, and references.
Verify one final implementation plan remains active.
Verify duplicate naming document is non-authoritative.
expected_results: Superseded source plans do not remain active
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify cutover occurred only per registry and with rollback evidence
procedure: For each cutover registry, verify qualification evidence, sustained green state, approval, atomic metadata changes, and tested rollback.
Reject global or partial inconsistent cutover.
expected_results: Every canonical registry has a cutover decision and qualification report.
Rollback was executed successfully in an isolated test.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify retired or superseded artifacts remain auditable
procedure: Verify superseded artifacts are retained or archived according to policy, contain pointers, and are excluded from routing.
Verify no archived content is used as current authority.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify born-canonical and no-declared-authority registries have substitute evidence
procedure: For each registry lacking a predecessor authority, verify a governed basis: declared authority, born-canonical with substitute validators, or retirement.
Run substitute validators against implementation/configuration evidence.
expected_results: Born-canonical registries have passing external evidence checks
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify current routing file and document authority agree
procedure: Cross-validate every active routing reference against doc authority and authority manifest.
Detect missing, duplicate, or stale paths.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Point a fixture route to a superseded document.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

verification_gates.on_fail: Do not declare canonical authority or completed governance for any affected fact group or registry.

verification_gates.title: Parity, conflict, and evidence quality
verification_gates.purpose: Verify the registry facts were reconciled honestly, with deterministic parity and traceable evidence.

checks.title: Verify parity authority map coverage and doc-authority consistency
procedure: Validate parity authority map schema.
Cross-validate mapped authorities with doc authority.
Verify every registry has an explicit coverage classification or substitute evidence basis.
expected_results: Unavailable authorities are explicit and fail closed
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify parity engine is deterministic, read-only, and closed-taxonomy
procedure: Run parity twice against identical inputs.
Verify stable parity IDs and verdict taxonomy.
Monitor authored files for writes.
expected_results: Parity IDs stable across value changes where identity is unchanged
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Remove an authority fixture or inject an unsupported comparison.
negative_test.expected_failure: Result is authority_unavailable or blocking tooling error, never agree.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify baseline ratchet integrity

requirement_sources.summary: Baseline may shrink only through evidenced resolution

procedure: Compare baseline changes to resolution records and parity IDs.
Detect new, known, resolved, and stale entries.
Verify tooling never auto-appends.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Delete a still-divergent parity ID or add a nonexistent ID in a fixture baseline.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify conflict burn-down evidence quality
procedure: For every resolved divergence and closed conflict item, inspect evidence class, path, commit SHA, locator, observation time, and parity linkage.
Enforce confidence ceilings and authority-only labeling.
expected_results: E3-only records marked derived_from_authority_only and not high confidence
Baseline removals have one-for-one resolution links
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify real code defects were not hidden as documentation fixes
procedure: Inspect contract and integration conflict resolutions.
Verify genuine producer/consumer mismatches are recorded and referred as code defects rather than normalized away.
expected_results: Known real integration defects have code issue references.
No conflicting implementation states are collapsed into false agreement.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify schema and comparison gaps are closed or explicitly accepted
procedure: Review unrepresentable and not_comparable verdicts.
Verify schema extensions or deterministic comparisons were implemented where required.
Verify accepted differences have written rationale and approval.
expected_results: Every not_comparable item is resolved or approved
No not_comparable item is counted as agree
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify sustained green evidence is per registry
procedure: Inspect generated stability report and commit sequence.
Verify the threshold is met with normal repository activity and no hidden baseline resets.
expected_results: Each cutover registry meets configured consecutive-green threshold.
Green streak is tied to commit SHAs.
Baseline was not regenerated during streak.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Run anti-transcription honesty audit
procedure: Calculate resolution evidence-class distribution.
Review suspicious clusters of authority-only resolutions.
Sample records to verify implementation or artifact evidence where claimed.
expected_results: Authority-copying patterns are explained or remediated.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Block cutover for affected registries and restore any improperly removed baseline entries.

verification_gates.title: Determinism and drift resistance
verification_gates.purpose: Prove repeatable generation, complete no-write checking, cross-version compatibility, and clean-clone reproducibility.

checks.title: Prove two-build byte determinism

requirement_sources.summary: Same approved inputs produce byte-identical outputs

procedure: Run the complete generation pipeline twice from unchanged approved inputs.
Hash all managed outputs after each run.
expected_results: Managed output hash set is identical.
Second build produces no Git diff.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Introduce a nondeterministic fixture field such as current time into a test renderer.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Prove no-write check mode covers every managed output

requirement_sources.summary: Check mode writes nothing and checks all outputs

procedure: Snapshot file hashes and mtimes.
Run check mode.
Compare hashes, mtimes, and working tree.
Mutate one file from each output class and repeat.
expected_results: Each mutated output class causes nonzero exit without rewrite.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

negative_test.mutation: Tamper each managed output class one at a time.
negative_test.expected_failure: Check mode fails and leaves the tampered file unchanged.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

checks.title: Verify cross-platform path and newline normalization
procedure: Inspect manifests and generated content for absolute paths, backslashes, host-specific paths, or inconsistent newlines.
Compare supported-platform outputs where CI evidence exists.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify Python 3.9 and 3.11 execution evidence
procedure: Run required registry and verification tests under Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Compare generated bytes and results.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify dynamic counts are governed rather than hardcoded
procedure: Scan validators, plans, and CI for prohibited mutable count literals used as permanent acceptance constants.
Verify governed expected-count files or computed counts are used.
expected_results: Intentional count changes have evidence and review
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify clean-clone reproducibility
procedure: Create a fresh clone at the target commit.
Install declared dependencies only.
Run generation, validation, tests, and check mode.
Verify no uncommitted required inputs.
expected_results: Clean clone completes all required commands.
No hidden local dependency is required.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify deterministic verification artifacts
procedure: Generate the obligation matrix and Markdown checklist twice from the same plan and specification.
Compare bytes and manifests.
expected_results: No runtime timestamp appears in deterministic content unless supplied as input.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Reject deterministic-delivery claims and block cutover or final completion until byte stability is restored.

verification_gates.title: Negative and mutation testing
verification_gates.purpose: Prove that the controls detect invalid states rather than merely passing the current repository.

checks.title: Verify registry structural controls fail correctly
procedure: Run isolated mutations for malformed JSONL, duplicate IDs, invalid IDs, unresolved references, and authority-state violations.
expected_results: Every mutation produces the expected nonzero result and diagnostic.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify parity and baseline controls fail correctly
procedure: Mutate authority availability, registry-only/authority-only records, field divergence, unrepresentable fields, new divergence, resolved divergence, and stale baseline cases.
expected_results: Expected verdict and blocking behavior occur for every case.
No mutation is silently normalized to agree.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify authority overlap and routing drift controls fail correctly
procedure: Introduce duplicate active owner, missing fact owner, stale routing pointer, and routing/doc-authority conflict in fixtures.
expected_results: Each condition blocks with a specific diagnostic.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify write-scope and generated-file protections fail correctly
procedure: Add an unauthorized path to a fixture diff.
Hand-edit generated artifacts.
Run scope and drift validators.
expected_results: Validators do not auto-repair during check.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify module naming and folder controls fail correctly
procedure: Mutate locator, symbol rendering, .module-id, container role, manifest name, and cross-module nesting in fixture trees.
expected_results: Every invalid structure blocks with module/path-specific diagnostics.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify path coverage and migration-map controls fail correctly
procedure: Remove a tracked path from the destination matrix.
Add a move outside the approved rename map.
Create a path-consumer mismatch.

required_evidence.description: Path and migration map mutation results

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify rollback controls detect incomplete reversibility

requirement_sources.summary: Every cutover and move has tested rollback

procedure: Remove or corrupt one rollback entry in a fixture.
Run rollback validator and simulated rollback.
expected_results: Incomplete rollback leaves detectable diff and fails.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify downstream consumer dependency is real

requirement_sources.summary: At least one consumer must truly depend on registry data

procedure: Delete or invalidate a required registry record in an isolated branch/fixture.
Run the declared downstream consumer CI or build.
expected_results: Consumer fails for the expected reason.
Failure is not caused by unrelated test setup.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Treat the corresponding positive control as unproven and block the affected completion claim.

verification_gates.title: Migration and rollback
verification_gates.purpose: Verify the 34-module physical migration is complete, evidence-backed, bounded, behavior-preserving, and reversible.

checks.title: Verify 34-module identity and locator ratification
procedure: Validate active module count, ID/symbol/locator uniqueness, aliases, F4 governance, and owner approval.
Verify older catalogs are explicitly classified.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify module structure policies are complete and consistent

requirement_sources.summary: Container, profile, packaging, schema, state, and global-root policy

procedure: Validate container inventories, profiles, root artifacts, packaging, schema placement, state contract, and global-root policy.
Verify every module has exactly one primary profile and evidence-backed extensions.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify every tracked repository path has one approved disposition

requirement_sources.summary: Complete path ownership and destination mapping

procedure: Compare Git tracked-file inventory to the file-destination matrix.
Verify multi-owner, unmapped, generated, runtime-only, archive, and global paths have governed dispositions.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify target tree and migration artifacts are generated from approved inputs

requirement_sources.summary: Generated target tree, rename, impact, and rollback maps

procedure: Regenerate target tree, rename map, impact report, rollback map, and validation report.
Trace inputs to approved identity, policy, and destination records.
expected_results: Every move has an inverse rollback entry.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify path consumers and runtime-constrained names
procedure: Scan code, CI, packaging, docs, Docker, PowerShell, MT4 deployment, MQL4 includes, and runtime file references.
Verify runtime-constrained folders remain conventional.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify representative pilot migrations
procedure: Confirm pilot set covers pure Python, Python/MT4, UI/backend, and shared-kernel or low-implementation classes.
Verify imports, tests, packaging, CI paths, MT4 paths where applicable, structure validation, and rollback.
expected_results: Every pilot rollback was executed successfully.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify bounded wave execution and no-big-bang discipline
procedure: Review wave maps, PRs, changed paths, validations, and rollback checkpoints.
Verify each wave began from green master and did not exceed approved scope.
expected_results: Each wave has independent validation and rollback evidence
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify post-migration repository structure and behavior
procedure: Run final structure validators, tracked-path coverage, stale-path scan, imports, packaging, tests, deployment checks, and MT4-specific checks where applicable.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Block migration completion and isolate the affected module, path, or wave. Use the tested rollback path where necessary.

verification_gates.title: CI, clean clone, and operational readiness
verification_gates.purpose: Verify the delivered registry system is enforced, reproducible, consumed, supportable, and resistant to future drift.

checks.title: Verify required CI checks executed and passed

requirement_sources.summary: Startup-blocked CI is not passing evidence

procedure: Resolve required check names from current ruleset.
Verify checks ran on the target commit and completed successfully.
Reject skipped, neutral, cancelled, or startup-blocked jobs.
expected_results: No missing or renamed required check is silently ignored.

required_evidence.description: Required check runs and job details

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify CI controls are blocking at the correct maturity state

requirement_sources.summary: No advisory escape hatch at completion

procedure: Inspect workflow configuration and registry maturity/cutover state.
Verify structural and parity regression checks are blocking, and canonical registries have no report-only escape path.
expected_results: Canonical registries have no report-only bypass.
Advisory findings are limited to explicitly nonblocking scope.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify at least one real downstream registry consumer
procedure: Identify downstream consumers and their registry inputs.
Trace build-manifest provenance.
Use mutation evidence from Gate 07 to prove dependency.
expected_results: At least one CI or build consumer uses registry output.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify registry operations runbook is executable
procedure: Follow the runbook in a disposable branch to add, change, and retire fixture records.
Verify schema, reference, generation, PR evidence, and non-deletion retirement rules.
expected_results: No undocumented mandatory step is required.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify CODEOWNERS and PR templates enforce registry workflow
procedure: Inspect CODEOWNERS and PR templates.
Verify authored registry paths require appropriate review and templates request generated diff/evidence.
expected_results: Canonical authored registry paths are covered.
Required review cannot be bypassed under normal rules.
Registry PR template contains evidence and generated-diff sections.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify clean-clone and disaster recovery instructions
procedure: Execute clean-clone workflow and documented recovery/rollback steps in isolation.
Verify authoritative inputs can regenerate outputs and status.
expected_results: Recovery instructions restore a valid system.
No hidden workstation state is required.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify final supersession sweep
procedure: Audit architecture, module, process, contract, registry, migration, and plan documents against doc authority.
Classify each as current authority, registered retained authority, superseded with pointer, archived, or generated.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify ongoing drift prevention after completion
procedure: Review scheduled/triggered validation, required checks, ownership, change policy, and generated artifacts.
Verify future changes must pass the same controls.
expected_results: Ongoing validation is integrated into normal PR flow.
No manual-only critical drift control remains without assigned owner.
Change policy names escalation and rollback paths.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Do not declare the registry system ready for normal operation.

verification_gates.title: Final declaration and evidence seal
verification_gates.purpose: Make the final completion decision only after complete coverage, passing controls, honest claims, and sealed evidence.

checks.title: Reconcile all blocking and high-severity verdicts
procedure: Aggregate check verdicts by severity and gate.
Validate required evidence references and freshness.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify 100 percent obligation coverage and resolution
procedure: Validate coverage matrix and per-obligation verdicts.
Require every obligation to be pass or validly approved not_applicable.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Run anti-false-completion audit
procedure: Evaluate every anti-false-completion control against repository and evidence.
Sample high-risk claims: authority, parity, determinism, CI, cutover, migration, consumer use, and supersession.
expected_results: Every sampled claim has direct evidence
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify residual findings are scoped and do not contradict completion
procedure: Inventory all open issues, warnings, accepted exceptions, and not_applicable verdicts.
Determine whether any contradicts target-plan definition of done or claimed cutover/migration scope.
expected_results: Accepted residuals have owner, rationale, scope, and follow-up
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify final report is evidence-backed and schema-valid
procedure: Validate final verification report schema.
Resolve every evidence_id to the evidence manifest and hash.
Verify no result is embedded only as unsupported prose.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Verify owner completion approval
procedure: Present the final report, coverage, residuals, hashes, and rollback status to the repository owner.
Record explicit approve, reject, or defer decision.
expected_results: Final approval exists for a completed status.
Approval identifies target plan hash, target commit, verification spec hash, and report hash.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Seal the verification evidence bundle
procedure: Compute hashes for all run artifacts.
Write final evidence manifest and verify it.
Mark the bundle immutable by policy or release tag.
expected_results: Target commit, plan hash, spec hash, and report hash recorded.

required_evidence.description: Release tag or immutable commit reference

checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

checks.title: Publish an honest final status

requirement_sources.summary: Claims that must not be made without evidence

procedure: Compare final status language to actual verdicts, cutover states, parity baselines, CI execution, and migration scope.
Reject overstated claims.
expected_results: Published status is one of verified_complete, verified_incomplete, or verification_blocked.
Every material claim is supported by a report field and evidence IDs.
checks.remediation_on_fail: Stop verification for the affected scope, preserve evidence, and open a focused remediation item before re-running the check.

verification_gates.on_fail: Publish verified_incomplete or verification_blocked. Never publish verified_complete.

verified_complete_requires: All verification gates pass in order.
All blocker and high checks pass.
All 1405 obligations are mapped and resolved.
All required evidence is fresh and hash-resolvable.
Anti-false-completion controls detect no unresolved blocking pattern.
verified_incomplete_when: Verification executed but one or more required results failed.
Required migration, cutover, consumer, runbook, or supersession work is incomplete.
verification_blocked_when: Verification cannot bind to the exact plan or commit.
Required routing/authority references are unavailable for dependent conclusions.
Required CI or runtime evidence cannot execute.
Evidence integrity or freshness cannot be established.
completion_policy.residual_finding_rule: Residual findings may remain only when they do not contradict the claimed scope or any definition-of-done obligation and have owner, rationale, severity, and follow-up.
completion_policy.status_claim_rule: The published status must use exactly one final_status_value and must not be softened or overstated.

semantic_validation_rules.rule: All gate_id, check_id, rule_id, control_id, and obligation-generation rule IDs are unique.
Every gate dependency resolves to an earlier gate and the gate graph is acyclic.
Gate order values are unique and contiguous from 0 through 10.
Every phase_ref resolves to a phase_id in the bound target plan.
Every requirement source JSON Pointer resolves in the bound target plan or this specification, as identified by source_type.
The sum of expected_counts equals expected_total_obligations.
Expected counts exactly match counts extracted from the bound target plan.
Every blocker or high check has fail_closed true and at least one required evidence item.
Every check has at least one requirement source, procedure step, expected result, and remediation action.
Every required package artifact has a unique package_order and path.
The specification contains no run verdict, pass count, evidence ID, or completion claim.
Target plan ID, version, byte size, and SHA-256 match the bound file.
Every evidence_type used by a check exists in evidence_contract.evidence_types.
Every check allowed_verdicts equals the closed verdict taxonomy.
Every enforcement claim has a negative test in the same or a referenced check.

before_verification: Read this specification, its schema, the bound target plan, and the project routing authority.
Resolve required references and freeze repository context.
Validate the specification and target binding.
Generate and validate the plan-obligation matrix before checking implementation.
during_verification: Use fresh evidence from the bound target commit.
Record exact commands, outputs, hashes, decisions, and blockers.
Never edit implementation, authority, baseline, or evidence to force a pass.
Run mutations and rollbacks only in isolated fixtures or disposable worktrees.
Stop downstream completion claims when a blocker/high check fails or is blocked.
after_verification: Validate the final report and every evidence reference.
Run anti-false-completion and final claim audits.
Record owner approval, rejection, or deferral.
Publish only an allowed final status.
prohibited_behavior: Silently changing the verification specification or target binding.
Suppressing failed controls through baseline or authority edits.
Claiming complete with incomplete obligation coverage.

global_fail_closed_conditions: Target plan hash, ID, version, or byte size does not match.
Specification schema or semantic validation fails.
Required routing or authority reference is missing for a dependent conclusion.
Repository target commit cannot be frozen.
Plan-obligation count or pointer validation fails.
A blocker/high check fails or is blocked.
Required evidence is missing, stale, unverifiable, or hash-invalid.
A negative test does not fail as expected.
A rollback test does not restore valid state.
An authority or baseline was modified to force a pass.
Final report contains unsupported or overstated claims.
required_response: Preserve raw outputs, diffs, and hashes.
Classify the failure by gate, check, obligation, and affected scope.
Open or record a focused remediation item.
Do not alter evidence or specification in the same run.
Publish verified_incomplete or verification_blocked as appropriate.

integrity.serialization: UTF-8, LF newlines, 2-space indentation, trailing newline.
integrity.self_hash_policy: Do not embed this specification's own SHA-256. Record it in the release/evidence manifest to avoid self-reference.
